name: Cornish Hens and Plums on Baby Spinach
recipeIngredient: Fresh baby spinach, washed and patted dry
5 ripe red plums, halved and pitted
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the balsamic vinaigrette several hours or up to a day before serving by combining olive oil, balsamic vinegar, water, ground cumin, salt, and ground black pepper in a pint jar with a tight-fitting lid. Shake well and cover; set aside.
2. About 1 hour before serving, preheat the grill to medium heat. Lightly coat the grill rack with nonstick cooking spray.
3. On a cutting board, finely chop the plum halves.
4. In a bowl, mix the chopped plums, onion, balsamic vinegar, honey, ground cumin, salt, celery seeds, and pepper.
5. Uncover the breasts of the Cornish game hens (2 lbs) and spread one-quarter of the plum mixture under each hen.
6. Place the hens, breast-side down, on the grill rack about 6 inches above the heat. Grill for about 20 minutes until golden brown.
7. Thread the remaining 8 plum halves onto several thin metal or soaked bamboo skewers.
8. Flip the hens and continue grilling on the remaining side for an additional 15 to 20 minutes, or until juices run clear when a fork is inserted into the thickest part of a hen.
9. Brush the grilled plums with some balsamic vinaigrette and grill for an additional 3 minutes on each side.
10. When ready to serve, place the baby spinach in an even layer on a large serving platter. Arrange the hens on top of the spinach. Place the grilled plums alongside the hens. Pour any remaining balsamic vinaigrette over the dish and serve immediately.
